events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need analyze scenario: residential HVAC-R, thermostats & sequences, observed field condition: aux heat locked on. Work tested/inspected. Reference mfr manuals. Need compare field condition against referenced document. Determine decision fail because aux heat locked on is defective/non-compliant? Need assess risk: aux heat locked on can cause high energy cost, overheating? In heat pump systems, auxiliary heat (electric resistance) locked on when not called is a malfunction - could be thermostat wiring/config or sequencer issue. Risk likely high if it causes overheating or fire? Residential: aux heat stuck on can cause excessive heat, high bills, potential equipment damage, but not immediate critical maybe high. Component condition installed-defective. Decision fail. Need findings: auxiliary heat energized continuously/locked on contrary to control sequence; thermostat/sequencer not operating per manufacturer. Actions: verify wiring, settings, sequencer/relay; replace defective thermostat/sequencer/relay; retest heating stages and lockouts. References: mfr manuals, maybe NEC? But only mfr manuals given. Could include IRC? The instructions say apply reference material rather than quoting mechanically. Use mfr manuals. Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 90 since tested/inspected. value 0. sound_source empty? Use "" maybe. confidence 0.9. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Need ensure valid JSON. No prose outside JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat is locked on and not cycling with thermostat/heat-pump demand",
    "Control sequence does not match manufacturer operating instructions",
    "Potential for overheating, excessive energy use, and equipment damage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize auxiliary heat circuit until defect is corrected",
    "Inspect thermostat wiring, configuration, and heat-stage staging per manufacturer manual",
    "Test sequencer/relay or auxiliary heat contactor for stuck-closed condition",
    "Replace defective thermostat, sequencer, or relay as needed",
    "Re-run heating test and verify auxiliary heat only operates when properly called"
  ],
  "rationale": "Auxiliary heat locked on is an installed-defective, non-compliant control condition. Manufacturer sequences require auxiliary heat to operate only on demand; continuous operation indicates a wiring, thermostat, or sequencer fault that must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": ["mfr manuals"]
}
